name: DNA Repair Enhancement surveillance pass for June 10, 2026

scope.question: What changed for DNA Repair Enhancement since the May 31, 2026 baseline publication, and does any change justify a public update?

summary: Same-window surveillance found one material public update candidate: a June 2026 Nat Aging DREAM repression paper linking repair-repressor biology to somatic mutation burden, mammalian lifespan, human Alzheimer's disease association data, and lower mutation accumulation in DREAM-knockout mouse brain. Because the source already exists in the public data, the staged bundle attaches its study/finding to DNA Repair Enhancement and refreshes the track outlook without upgrading stage, momentum, or confidence. Known trial registries did not yield new result-bearing updates, and broader NAD/nucleotide and disease-boundary hits point to a follow-on coverage-repair need.

materiality_decision.rationale: PMID 42230995 is a post-baseline primary source directly relevant to DNA repair regulation and somatic mutation burden, so the public support map should include it. The source is preclinical/comparative and does not justify a stage upgrade. Discovery also exposed thin systemic repair-regulator, active-registry, and taxonomy-boundary coverage that should be repaired after this staged update is reviewed.

search_log.summary: Returned 15 IDs. PMID 42230995 was staged as a material same-window source. Close hits included exogenous nucleotides with epigenetic/telomere endpoints, a DNA-repair-targeting review, endothelial DNA damage disease biology, DNA glycosylase Alzheimer's review context, and skin-cancer mechanism papers that did not change the public track read.

search_log.query: nicotinamide AND (skin cancer OR DNA repair OR photodamage), 2026/05/31 onward
search_log.summary: Returned 5 IDs, mostly oncology or editorial context. No new result-bearing nicotinamide DNA-repair or skin-chemoprevention trial source was found.

search_log.query: (photolyase OR "DNA repair enzyme") AND (skin OR sunscreen OR actinic), 2026/05/31 onward
(nicotinamide riboside OR nicotinamide mononucleotide OR NMN OR NR) AND (DNA repair OR PARP OR genomic instability) AND human, 2026/05/31 onward
search_log.summary: Returned 0 same-window direct human NAD-precursor DNA-repair endpoint records.
Known NR older-adult registry remained completed, last update posted 2016-12-01, with no ClinicalTrials.gov results section. The PubMed article remains the result-bearing public source.

search_log.query: DNA repair aging; nicotinamide DNA repair; DNA repair enzyme skin; photolyase sunscreen; nicotinamide mononucleotide older adults
search_log.summary: Broad registry searches found no new posted result-bearing DNA-repair-enhancement trial. Close registry hits included NCT06465602 nicotinamide falls/frailty, NCT06208527 NADage, NCT07144527 NMN exercise tolerance, NCT06592859 NMN in middle-aged/elderly people, NR ataxia-telangiectasia registries, and topical DNA repair enzyme skin studies; these were no-results, wrong-scope, already covered in NAD/redox, or historical.
UMIN listed the NMN older-men trial as completed, last modified 2021-03-02, with unpublished registry results; the PubMed article remains the result-bearing public source.
Direct machine retrieval of ANZCTR trial pages was blocked by a managed challenge. PubMed records and official registry URLs confirm the ACTRN identifiers, and no new result-bearing source was found.

trial_watch_checks.summary: ClinicalTrials.gov still has no registry results section and was last updated in 2016; the published NR trial remains already represented as indirect NAD-linked context without DNA repair endpoints.
UMIN listed the NMN older-men trial as completed and unpublished in-registry; the published article remains already represented as indirect NAD-linked context without DNA repair endpoints.
Direct ANZCTR retrieval was blocked, but the ONTRAC PubMed report remains the result-bearing source and no new registry-linked result was found.
Direct ANZCTR retrieval was blocked, but the ONTRANS PubMed report remains the result-bearing null source and no new registry-linked result was found.

excluded_sources.title: Urate-Related Genetic Risk Modulates the Anti-Aging Effects of Exogenous Nucleotides
excluded_sources.reason: Human nucleotide supplementation moved DNAmAge and telomere endpoints in a secondary analysis, but it did not directly measure DNA repair enhancement and should be handled, if at all, in coverage repair or a nucleotide/telomere boundary pass.

excluded_sources.title: Therapeutic targeting of DNA repair pathway dysregulation in aging, cancer, and neurodegeneration
excluded_sources.reason: Recent review is useful for future source completeness but is not primary result-bearing evidence for the staged surveillance update.

excluded_sources.title: Endothelial DNA Damage Orchestrates Cardio-Kidney-Metabolic Dysfunction Through Endothelin-1 Signaling
excluded_sources.reason: Disease-mechanism and mouse perturbation source is relevant to systemic DNA damage biology but was available before the baseline review window and does not by itself change the current track outlook.

excluded_sources.title: The role and therapeutic potential of DNA glycosylases in Alzheimer's disease
excluded_sources.reason: Review of DNA glycosylases in Alzheimer's disease is disease-boundary context, not a new treated-aging or direct track-update result.

excluded_sources.title: Photodynamic therapy and nicotinamide-complementary strategies in cutaneous carcinogenesis
excluded_sources.reason: Editorial context did not add primary DNA-repair-enhancement evidence beyond the existing nicotinamide skin-cancer records.

excluded_sources.title: Nicotinamide mononucleotide supplementation for exercise tolerance improvement in healthy older adults
excluded_sources.reason: Already represented in the NAD and Redox Restoration public layer; it has no posted results and no direct DNA repair endpoint.

excluded_sources.title: Intervention of nicotinamide mononucleotide in middle-aged and elderly people
excluded_sources.reason: Recruiting no-results NAD/NMN registry is more direct for NAD and Redox Restoration than DNA Repair Enhancement, and it has no posted DNA repair result.

excluded_sources.title: Nicotinamide supplementation improves physical performance in older adults at risk of falls
excluded_sources.reason: Nicotinamide frailty/falls registry has unknown status and no posted results; no DNA repair endpoint was identified in the registry summary.

excluded_sources.title: Nicotinamide riboside ataxia-telangiectasia registries
excluded_sources.reason: Disease-specific DNA repair syndrome registries are useful boundary context but not broad aging-track evidence, and no posted results were identified.

notes: The staged DREAM update broadens the repair-regulator biology but should not be read as a human intervention signal.

The public read remains limited by skin-local human evidence, null transplant-recipient nicotinamide results, indirect NAD-precursor evidence, and unresolved safety/durability questions for systemic repair manipulation.

ANZCTR pages were not machine-readable during this pass because the registry returned a managed challenge; the ACTRN identifiers remain traceable through the published ONTRAC and ONTRANS trial reports.
next_actions: Run evidence review lanes for the submitted surveillance bundle before editorial approval.
After publication, run coverage repair for DNA Repair Enhancement to handle systemic repair-regulator, nucleotide/NAD boundary, recent review, and disease-specific DNA repair syndrome context.
